subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall narrative with logical progression through rationale, data collection, model selection, prediction, and repayment analysis
Well-defined structure: contents page, consistent headings, numbered tables/figures, and a clear introductionâbodyâconclusion layout
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and lengthy explanations of basic concepts reduce conciseness
Appendix and some figure captions are only partially integrated into the main discussion
Minor mismatches between table of contents entries and actual page numbers
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments indicate a coherent and logically developed exploration (band 3) with good organization (band 3) but only mostly relevant content (band 2). On balance, the work meets the descriptor for a score of 3: it is coherent and well organized, though not yet exceptionally concise or fully integrated in all parts.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of mathematical notation and language throughout most of the exploration
Effective use of multiple representations (scatter plots, fitted curves, tables, regression outputs) to support modelling choices
Logical structure of mathematical argumentation, with clear steps from data to model selection, parameter determination, and extrapolation
weaknesses: Axes and units are occasionally missing or unclear on figures
Minor notation slips (e.g., mixing italics/roman, inconsistent subscript syntax)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although strand-level scores were largely band 3, the student's use of notation is almost entirely consistent, representations are effective and well referenced, and the logical flow is clear. These qualities meet the band 4 descriptors: mostly consistent language, effective multiple representations, and good logical clarity, justifying a score of 4.

strengths: Significant evidence of independent thinking in comparing logistic vs. exponential fits and introducing wage-index modelling
Clear personal narrative linking mathematical work to the studentâs own university decision context
Good display of initiative in exploring repayment timelines and alternative scenarios
weaknesses: Approach, while personal, remains within familiar SL modelling pathways without highly innovative techniques
Creativity is solid but not exceptional in the context of SL expectations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall into the band 2 descriptor (âsignificant evidenceâ, âclear personal approachâ, âgood level of creativityâ). The work goes beyond procedural treatment but does not yet show the outstanding independence or exceptional innovation required for a band 3 score.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on model limitations (e.g., logistic under-prediction, fixed WPI growth)
Thoughtful discussion of why certain models were rejected or selected based on RÂ² and economic considerations
Clear evaluation of outcomes with implications (e.g., repayment period surprises, scholarship recommendations)
weaknesses: Reflection is episodic rather than woven consistently throughout
Analysis stops short of deep probing (no residual analysis, limited statistical validation)
Evaluation remains qualitative without alternative recalculations or comprehensive future projections
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments align with the band 2 level: meaningful but not substantial reflection, thoughtful but not deeply critical analysis, and clear but not comprehensive evaluation. These place the work squarely at a score of 2.

strengths: Expert and comprehensive use of SL mathematicsâincluding logistic regression, exponential modelling, geometric sequences and seriesâand adept integration of manual derivations with GeoGebra outputs
Sophisticated application of multiple methods to validate and compare models (manual vs. software fits)
Thorough integration of mathematical technology to enhance precision and confirm results across sections
weaknesses: Minor arithmetic and rounding inconsistencies between tables and model inputs
Occasional premature rounding in intermediate steps that slightly affects final figures
Inconsistent notation for series sums (rÂ² vs. rË£)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite a few small inaccuracies, the student demonstrates expert-level use of course-relevant mathematics throughout: selecting and applying advanced modelling techniques, validating results with technology, and producing coherent parameter derivations. This holistic command of SL mathematics meets the band 6 descriptors, warranting the maximum score of 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are generally sequential: data â model choice â parameter calculation â extrapolation. Working is shown and steps are explained so the reader can follow. Minor algebraic slips and some lengthy verbal digressions limit absolute clarity, but overall structure is good.

Derivations are generally sound; parameters are calculated and checked, and repayment algebra is correct. Minor inconsistencies (rounding discrepancies, using r instead of RÂ² symbols, one currency-conversion omission) indicate âgood understanding with minor errorsâ rather than âthorough and completely correct.â

Scatter plots, fitted curves, tables and a contents outline are used effectively to illustrate data, models and results. The representations are well chosen and usually referenced in the text, though axes and units are not always labelled with full clarity, preventing a move to the highest band.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: rationale, data collection, model selection, prediction, and repayment analysis. Transitions between sections are mostly smooth and the mathematical development (e.g., rejecting linear, testing logistic, then exponential) is generally logical. Minor digressions and some lengthy explanations of basic concepts occasionally interrupt the flow, so it does not reach the âexcellentâ descriptor.

Choosing between logistic and exponential fits, introducing wage indices and geometric growth, and computing repayment years exhibit creativity and initiative beyond a purely procedural treatment. However, the ideas remain within familiar SL territory, so they align with a âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ level.

There is thoughtful discussion of model suitability (rejecting linear/logarithmic, comparing RÂ² values), and acknowledgement of limitations such as fixed WPI or currency conversion. Analysis is sound but not deeply probing (no statistical validation, residual analysis), so it meets the âthoughtfulâ descriptor.

Outcomes are evaluated (repayment period too long, scholarships suggested, higher income scenario noted) and implications are mentioned, but recommendations stay qualitative and no alternative recalculation is attempted. This is a clear, yet not comprehensive, evaluation.

The student reflects on why logistic under-predicts, why 30 % may be unrealistic, and on the surprise at eight-year repayment. Reflection appears in several sections but is episodic rather than woven âthroughout,â fitting the âmeaningfulâ band.

Variables are defined, functions are written with parameters, and standard notation (e, rÂ², subscripts) is used in most places. Occasional slips (mixing italics/roman, inconsistent sub-script syntax) occur, but overall the language and symbols are appropriate and mostly consistent.

A clear personal narrative underpins the task (own university decision, 30 % repayment rule, preferred six-year horizon). This shows a personal approach, yet the structure still follows a textbook modelling pathway with little innovation, so the evidence is âclearâ but not âhighly innovative.â

Most content is relevant, but explanations of elementary ideas (e.g., why dollars are rounded, definitions of RÂ², or repetition of geometric-series formula) add unnecessary length. At 20 pages the text could be noticeably more concise; therefore it meets âmostly relevantâ rather than âconcise throughout.â

Exponential/logistic modelling, geometric sequences, series sums and logarithmic solving are all relevant to the research question and applied correctly to derive predictions and repayment periods. The mathematics is integrated effectively rather than in isolated sections.

The student selects a personally meaningful problem, compares alternative models, and justifies choices, demonstrating significant but not outstanding independent thought. The mathematics applied is standard for SL and there is no original technique, so the work fits the âsignificantâ descriptor rather than âoutstanding.â

Headings, a contents page, numbered tables/figures, and a consistent two-part approach (tuition then income) give the work a clear structure. Introduction, body and conclusion are evident. However, the appendix and some figure captions are only partially integrated into the discussion, so the organisation is "well" rather than "exceptionally" executed.

Content (modelling, series) sits squarely at SL AA level and is fully appropriate. While not pushing to HL or advanced statistics, the selected mathematics is used in a way that is entirely commensurate with course expectations.

annotations.comment: The title is descriptive but overly long; consider condensing to improve conciseness and impact, focusing on key variables and research question.
Ensure the table of contents page numbers match the final document layout; discrepancies between TOC entries and actual page numbers may confuse readers.
rÂ² is mentioned before its formal definition in the text; consider defining the coefficient of determination immediately upon first use to aid reader comprehension.
The introduction contains overlapping rationale and aim statements; consolidating these ideas will improve conciseness and strengthen coherence.
In Table 1, the column heading 'University Tuition Fees' lacks currency notation; include 'AUD$' to clarify the units of measurement.
Table 2's heading 'University Tuition Fees' should include 'GBPÂ£' to specify the currency, aligning with the definition used in the Edinburgh data.
Figure 1 lacks axis labels and units for both axes; including these would significantly improve clarity and guide interpretation of the scatter plot.
The term 'logistics function' is used instead of 'logistic function'; adjust the terminology to reflect the correct mathematical model.
When manually deriving logistic parameters, establish a clear rounding policy (e.g., significant figures) to maintain consistency and minimize cumulative rounding errors.
Beyond comparing f(x) values, could you include residual analysis or plot residuals to more rigorously validate the logistic model's fit?
The reflection on why the logistic function underestimates later values demonstrates meaningful critical analysis of the model's limitations.
Using GeoGebra to derive a logistic regression provides a more accurate model by incorporating all data points, showing effective use of technology to enhance model precision.
The estimated carrying capacity of ~139,000 exceeds plausible future tuition; discuss practical constraints to justify this parameter in the context of university fees.
Comparing the manually derived and GeoGebra exponential functions confirms the reliability of manual methods and shows good use of multiple representations for validation.
The manual derivation uses 50,650 for the 2024 fee, but Table 1 lists 50,560; ensure consistency between data tables and model inputs to maintain accuracy.
When comparing total costs in AUD and GBP, consider converting to a common currency to make direct comparisons of affordability more meaningful.
Hourly wage calculations use rounded values prematurely; maintain full precision through intermediate steps and round only final annual or payment figures for consistency.
In the formula for Sâ you use rÂ² but should generalize rË£; ensure consistent notation when defining the geometric series for repayment.
Acknowledging that rounding to whole years may misrepresent the actual repayment duration demonstrates thoughtful reflection on reporting practical limitations.
The text reports f(16) as both AUD 51,019 and AUD 50,955; ensure consistency in calculation and rounding when reporting extrapolated figures.
Arbitrarily restricting the logistic function's domain when extrapolating may bias results; it would be better to select a model whose behavior aligns over the full domain of interest.
The description of exponential parameters a and b for Edinburgh could more explicitly link the numeric values to real-world tuition levels to enhance reader understanding.
The justification for selecting the exponential model for Edinburgh based on a higher rÂ² and economic context demonstrates substantive critical reflection and model evaluation.
Assuming a constant WPI growth rate for multiple years oversimplifies real wage variability; discussing potential deviations would strengthen the model's credibility.
The transition to full-time wage forecasting would benefit from a clearer statement of starting WPI assumptions and data sources at the point of model introduction.
